title: ‘A surreal experience’: Readers react to loud boom in Mass. caused by meteor
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A sonic boom from a meteor was heard across eastern Massachusetts, southern New Hampshire, and parts of Rhode Island and Connecticut.
Meteorologist confirmed a bolide meteor caused the noise, prompting reactions from confused residents who speculated about explosions.
89% of 285 Boston.com readers reported hearing or feeling the boom, with reactions ranging from amazement to fear.

title: Mass. town warns of ‘neurological symptoms’ spotted in animals
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: North Quabbin Regional Animal Control warns of wild animals in Athol displaying neurological symptoms like stumbling and seizures.
Officials are investigating the cause of these symptoms, primarily observed in downtown Athol and Pinedale and Pequig neighborhoods.
Pet owners are urged to update rabies and distemper vaccinations and report unusual wildlife behavior to Athol police.

title: ‘It’s a special feeling’: Mickey Gasper never gave up on his Major League dream, and now he’s reaping the rewards
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Mickey Gasper, hitting .333 this season, made his MLB debut with the Red Sox in August 2024.
Gasper's journey included six years in the Minors after being drafted in the 27th round by the Yankees.
Red Sox manager Chad Tracy praised Gasper's swing decisions and versatility in playing multiple positions.

title: Mass. family, Worcester woman identified as victims in deadly Virginia bus crash
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Greenfield family members and a Worcester woman identified as victims in a Virginia bus crash.
The bus crash on Interstate 81 killed five and injured 34; police charged driver Jing S. Dong.
Victims were involved with Providence Christian Academy; a GoFundMe was started for funeral expenses.

title: Connor Wong’s 3-RBI night helped power Red Sox win, and it couldn’t come at a better time for him individually
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Connor Wong contributed 3 RBIs in the Red Sox's win against Cleveland, strengthening his case as a secondary catcher.
Despite losing his primary role to Mickey Gasper, Wong hit a pivotal double after a home run call was overturned.
Wong currently holds a .245 batting average, outpacing teammate Carlos Narvaez's .210 this season.

title: Sonny Gray continues to be great addition to Red Sox rotation, earns 6th win vs. Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray pitched six innings, allowing one run and striking out seven in the Red Sox's 9-1 win over the Guardians.
Gray's ERA for May was 2.00 over five starts, improving his season ERA to 3.06.
The Red Sox are 7-3 in games started by Gray, who has a $31 million contract with an option for 2027.

title: Here’s the full lineup of athletes and celebrities involved in Drake Maye’s charity softball game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Drake Maye's charity softball game will take place at Polar Park in Worcester on Sunday.
Patriots coaches Mike Vrabel and Josh McDaniels will coach Team Patriots and Team Sox respectively.
Notable participants include Julian Edelman, Zdeno Chara, and comedians Matt Rife and Dave Portnoy.

title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The New England Patriots are reportedly close to acquiring Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown with a deal framework in place.
A potential trade on or after June 1 reduces the Eagles' salary cap hit from $43.5 million to $16 million.
Disagreements remain over the Patriots' reluctance to trade a first-round pick, with negotiations potentially dragging on.

title: Did you hear it? Here’s what we know about a big boom heard around Mass.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A loud boom was heard across Greater Boston on Saturday, speculated to be caused by a bolide meteor.
Meteorologists, including NBC10's Pete Bouchard, identified the source as a meteor reentering the atmosphere east of Boston.
Emergency services received numerous calls about the boom, but found no fires or explosions upon investigation.

title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning, leading to the Red Sox's 4-3 loss against the Guardians.
Brayan Bello was effective in bulk relief, pitching seven scoreless innings with five strikeouts and no walks.
The Red Sox have lost six of their last seven games, now 10 games under .500 and last in the AL East.
